title: rourea coriacea
intro: Rourea coriacea, also known as the "gum tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, leaves, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Rourea coriacea include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
